Article.ArticleTitle: Czech adaptation, construct validity and reliability of the leisure time physical activity questionnaire for people with disabilities.

Abstract.AbstractText: This study aimed to develop a Czech version of The Leisure Time Physical Activity Questionnaire for People with Disabilities (LTPAQ-D-CZ) and evaluate its construct validity using accelerometer-based measurements and reliability in manual wheelchair users (MWU).

The LTPAQ-D was translated into Czech using a standardized forward-backward translation process. Content validity was assessed through a focus group of six MWU who reviewed wording, clarity, and relevance. Test-retest reliability was evaluated over a 7-day interval in 23 MWU. For construct validity, 55 MWU wore an ActiGraph wGT3X-BT accelerometer continuously for seven days and completed a leisure time activity log, a sociodemographic questionnaire, and the LTPAQ-D-CZ.

The translation and validation process resulted in the LTPAQ-D-CZ. Significant positive correlations were observed between the LTPAQ-D-CZ and accelerometer-derived light, moderate, vigorous, moderate-to-vigorous, and total leisure time physical activity (rS = 0.306-0.659). Statistically significant differences were observed only for vigorous activity (Z = 3.61, p < 0.001) and strength training (Z = 2.89, p = 0.004). Test-retest reliability for total physical activity was high (ICC = 0.924, p < 0.001).
The LTPAQ-D-CZ demonstrated good validity and excellent test-retest reliability, supporting its use as a valid tool for measuring leisure time physical activity in MWU.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Faculty of Physical Culture, Palacký University Olomouc, Olomouc, Czech Republic.
Faculty of Physical Culture, Palacký University Olomouc, Olomouc, Czech Republic.
Faculty of Physical Culture, Palacký University Olomouc, Olomouc, Czech Republic.

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: The Czech version of the Leisure Time Physical Activity Questionnaire for People with Disabilities (LTPAQ-D-CZ) is a valid instrument for assessing leisure time physical activity in individuals with physical disabilities who use a manual wheelchair as a main means of mobility.This study is the first to evaluate the construct validity of the LTPAQ-D using accelerometer-based measurements.LTPAQ-D is a unique self-report tool suitable for manual wheelchair users with both congenital and acquired physical disabilities.
